misconception_text: Business students don't need to take PE courses
correct_answer: All undergrads need 2 PE units unless their major has an approved waiver; check with your advisor

misconception_text: KIN 2 counts as both PE units so you only need that one course
correct_answer: KIN 2 is 3 units of GE health content not an activity course; KIN 100 or KIN 105 fulfill the PE activity requirement

misconception_text: Any fitness class on campus counts toward the PE requirement
correct_answer: Only approved KIN activity courses listed in the catalog satisfy the 2-unit PE requirement

misconception_text: MIS students automatically get a PE waiver because it is a business major
correct_answer: Only specific concentrations have approved PE waivers; MIS students should verify with the Lucas College advising office

misconception_text: You can take PE courses after you have completed all other graduation requirements
correct_answer: PE courses can be taken any semester but are recommended early to avoid last-minute surprises

misconception_text: Any English course satisfies the GWAR requirement
correct_answer: Only designated WID courses like ENGL 100WB COMM 100W or LLD 100WB satisfy GWAR for business students

misconception_text: ENGL 1B fulfills the GWAR requirement
correct_answer: ENGL 1B is a lower-division GE course; GWAR requires a separate upper-division writing-in-the-discipline course

misconception_text: You can take ENGL 100WB before completing ENGL 2
correct_answer: ENGL 2 is a prerequisite for ENGL 100WB; students must complete it first with a C or better

misconception_text: GWAR only needs a passing grade D or above
correct_answer: GWAR courses require a grade of C or better to satisfy the requirement

misconception_text: Transfer students who completed a writing course at community college do not need GWAR
correct_answer: GWAR must be completed at SJSU unless an official articulation agreement covers the specific WID course

misconception_text: You can take BUS4 110A without first taking BUS4 92
correct_answer: BUS4 92 is a required prerequisite for BUS4 110A; you must pass it with C or better first

misconception_text: BUS4 110B can be taken at the same time as BUS4 110A
correct_answer: BUS4 110A must be completed before enrolling in BUS4 110B; they are sequential not concurrent

misconception_text: Any programming experience waives BUS4 92
correct_answer: Prior programming experience does not automatically waive BUS4 92; a formal substitution petition is required

misconception_text: BUS4 112 has no prerequisites so you can take it in your first semester
correct_answer: BUS4 112 requires BUS4 110A which itself requires BUS4 92 and BUS3 12; plan for at least two prior semesters

misconception_text: You can register for BUS4 119B without completing BUS3 189
correct_answer: BUS4 119B requires both BUS4 110B and BUS3 189 as prerequisites

misconception_text: BUS4 118D Big Data only requires BUS4 112
correct_answer: BUS4 118D requires BUS4 112 as required and BUS2 190 as recommended; plan both before enrolling

misconception_text: You can take BUS4 116 before BUS4 112
correct_answer: BUS4 116 Advanced Database requires BUS4 112 as a hard prerequisite

misconception_text: BUS4 118B Business Intelligence has no statistics prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS4 118B requires BUS2 190 which itself requires BUS2 90; statistics background is essential

misconception_text: BUS4 111 Networking can be taken before any other MIS course
correct_answer: BUS4 111 requires BUS4 110A as a prerequisite; take 110A first

misconception_text: The MIS practicum BUS4 119A can be taken in sophomore year
correct_answer: BUS4 119A requires BUS4 119B which depends on several earlier MIS courses; it is typically a senior-year course

misconception_text: BUS4 118W Web Computing only needs BUS4 110A
correct_answer: BUS4 118W requires BUS4 92 as required and BUS4 110A as recommended; plan both

misconception_text: BUS4 113 Systems Integration can be taken alongside BUS4 111
correct_answer: BUS4 113 requires BUS4 111 as a prerequisite; 111 must come first

misconception_text: BUS4 115 Cybersecurity has no networking prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS4 115 requires BUS4 111 Networking as a prerequisite

misconception_text: BUS4 117 ERP only needs database knowledge
correct_answer: BUS4 117 requires BUS4 112 as required and BUS4 110B as recommended

misconception_text: BUS4 114 IT Project Management can be taken after only BUS4 110A
correct_answer: BUS4 114 requires BUS4 110B Systems Analysis as a prerequisite

misconception_text: BUS3 189 Strategic Management can be taken in junior year
correct_answer: BUS3 189 requires all five upper-division business fundamentals courses; it is a senior capstone

misconception_text: BUS1 170 Finance has no accounting prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS1 170 requires both BUS1 20 Financial Accounting and BUS2 90 Business Statistics

misconception_text: BUS2 190 Business Analytics only requires calculus
correct_answer: BUS2 190 requires BUS2 90 Business Statistics as its prerequisite

misconception_text: You can substitute BUS3 10 for BUS3 12 if you are not a transfer student
correct_answer: BUS3 10 is only an approved substitute for BUS3 12 for transfer students; continuing students must take BUS3 12

misconception_text: STAT 95 can replace BUS2 90 for all students
correct_answer: STAT 95 can substitute for BUS2 90 only for transfer students who earned a C or better

misconception_text: BUS2 130 Marketing has no first-year experience prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS2 130 requires BUS3 12 Business First Year Experience as a prerequisite

misconception_text: BUS5 187 Global Business can be taken before marketing and management courses
correct_answer: BUS5 187 requires both BUS2 130 and BUS3 160 as prerequisites

misconception_text: BUS1 21 Managerial Accounting can be taken without financial accounting
correct_answer: BUS1 21 requires BUS1 20 Financial Accounting as a prerequisite

misconception_text: BUS2 193 Marketing Analytics has no statistics prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS2 193 requires both BUS2 130 and BUS2 190 as prerequisites

misconception_text: BUS5 140 Operations Management can be taken in freshman year
correct_answer: BUS5 140 requires BUS3 12 as a prerequisite; it is a sophomore-level course at earliest

misconception_text: ECON 1A counts toward GE Area 2 Math
correct_answer: ECON 1A satisfies GE Area 4 Social Sciences not Area 2 which requires a math or statistics course

misconception_text: You only need one science course to satisfy GE Area 5
correct_answer: GE Area 5 requires both a lecture and lab component; check specific course listings for approved combinations

misconception_text: PHIL 186 satisfies GE Area 3B Humanities
correct_answer: PHIL 186 satisfies GE Upper Division Area 4 not lower-division Area 3B

misconception_text: HIST 15 only counts for American Institutions and not for GE
correct_answer: HIST 15 satisfies both American Institutions US1 and the upper-division Integration requirement simultaneously

misconception_text: ECON 1B can substitute for ECON 1A in the major preparation
correct_answer: ECON 1A Macroeconomics and ECON 1B Microeconomics are separate required courses; neither substitutes for the other

misconception_text: Taking ENGL 2 satisfies both GE Area 1B and the GWAR requirement
correct_answer: ENGL 2 satisfies GE Area 1B but does NOT satisfy GWAR; a separate WID upper-division course is still required

misconception_text: Any upper-division course in Area 4 satisfies the American Institutions requirement
correct_answer: American Institutions requires specific US history and government courses; not all Area 4 courses qualify

misconception_text: MATH 32 Calculus III gives more GE credit than MATH 70
correct_answer: Both satisfy GE Area 2 equally; MATH 32 is more advanced but does not grant additional GE units

misconception_text: You can double-count a course for both GE Area 4 and American Institutions freely
correct_answer: Double counting is allowed only for designated courses like ECON 1B for Area 4 and specific history/government courses

misconception_text: SOCI 1 satisfies GE Area 5 Natural Sciences
correct_answer: SOCI 1 satisfies GE Area 4 Social Sciences and can also count for American Institutions US3

misconception_text: You only need 120 units to graduate regardless of grade distribution
correct_answer: You need 120 units AND a minimum 2.0 GPA AND all major requirements with C or better in major courses

misconception_text: Upper-division courses taken at a community college count toward the 40 upper-div requirement
correct_answer: Upper-division units must be earned at a 4-year institution; community college courses are lower-division

misconception_text: You need 30 units in residence but they can all be lower-division
correct_answer: Of the 30 units in residence 24 must be upper-division and 12 must be in your major

misconception_text: A D grade in a major course counts toward graduation
correct_answer: All major and concentration courses require a C or better; a D does not count and the course must be retaken

misconception_text: Transfer units from a 4-year university always count as upper-division
correct_answer: Upper-division designation depends on the course level at the originating institution; verify with the registrar

misconception_text: You can apply for graduation after completing 90 units
correct_answer: SJSU requires you to apply for graduation once you have 90 units but commencement occurs only after all requirements are met

misconception_text: PHIL 186 Ethics can be skipped if you already took an ethics course elsewhere
correct_answer: PHIL 186 is a required course in the upper-division integration block; transfer equivalents require a formal petition

misconception_text: You need only 12 upper-division units in your major for the BS degree
correct_answer: The BS requires 18 upper-division units in the major; the BA requires 12; MIS is a BS program

misconception_text: Internship credits from BUS4 119C automatically count toward concentration requirements
correct_answer: BUS4 119C counts as a University Elective not a required concentration course; verify with your advisor

misconception_text: You can retake a major course as Credit/No Credit to raise your GPA
correct_answer: Major courses required with a C or better must be taken for a letter grade; CR/NC does not satisfy the grade requirement

misconception_text: The MIS concentration only requires five courses
correct_answer: MIS requires 21 units of required courses plus a practicum plus 6 units of additional electives totaling 27 units

misconception_text: BUS4 118S Special Topics always covers the same content each semester
correct_answer: BUS4 118S content rotates each semester; check the current schedule of classes for the specific topic offered

misconception_text: You must take BUS4 119H Honors Practicum II to graduate with honors
correct_answer: BUS4 119H is one option for the practicum requirement; BUS4 119A regular practicum is equally valid for graduation

misconception_text: BUS4 118I AI for Social Good counts as a technical elective only
correct_answer: BUS4 118I satisfies the MIS additional elective requirement and also addresses ethical AI themes

misconception_text: You can complete the MIS concentration in one year if you take all courses concurrently
correct_answer: MIS courses have sequential prerequisites; BUS4 92 to 110A to 110B to 119B alone spans at minimum three semesters

misconception_text: Cloud computing BUS4 118A does not count toward any graduation requirement
correct_answer: BUS4 118A satisfies the MIS Concentration Additional Elective requirement; two such electives are needed

misconception_text: BUS5 181 Entrepreneurship is only for entrepreneurship majors
correct_answer: BUS5 181 is open to all business students and is an approved MIS additional elective option

misconception_text: There is only one valid course sequence for completing the MIS concentration
correct_answer: Multiple valid sequences exist; required and recommended prerequisites give scheduling flexibility

misconception_text: ERP systems knowledge BUS4 117 is optional and rarely tested in job interviews
correct_answer: SAP/ERP skills are highly sought by employers; BUS4 117 is a recommended elective for enterprise IT roles

misconception_text: IT Project Management BUS4 114 is only useful for project managers not developers
correct_answer: BUS4 114 teaches Agile and Scrum which are used daily by developers analysts and all IT team members

misconception_text: MATH 32 Calculus III is required for the MIS program
correct_answer: MATH 32 is not required; MATH 70 or a Calculus I equivalent satisfies the math requirement for MIS

misconception_text: Business Statistics BUS2 90 is the same course as STAT 95
correct_answer: STAT 95 may substitute for BUS2 90 for transfer students with C or better but they are different courses

misconception_text: You do not need any math background to take BUS2 90
correct_answer: BUS2 90 requires MATH 70 or equivalent as a prerequisite

misconception_text: MATH 71 is harder than MATH 70 and covers more material
correct_answer: MATH 71 is a calculus-track alternative to MATH 70 for business; they satisfy the same GE Area 2 requirement

misconception_text: ECON 101 Intermediate Microeconomics satisfies the same requirement as ECON 1B
correct_answer: ECON 101 is an upper-division elective; ECON 1B is a separate required major preparation course

misconception_text: You can declare MIS as a concentration in your senior year without issue
correct_answer: Declaring late means completing sequential MIS prerequisites is nearly impossible; declare by sophomore year

misconception_text: Completing 120 units is sufficient to walk at commencement
correct_answer: All academic GE major residence GWAR and PE requirements must be met; unit count alone is not sufficient

misconception_text: You only need to take BUS3 80 Legal Environment if you want to go into law
correct_answer: BUS3 80 is a required lower-division core course for all business students regardless of career goals

misconception_text: BUS4 91L Computer Tools is too basic and can be waived by demonstrating Excel skills
correct_answer: BUS4 91L is a required 1-unit core course; demonstrated skills do not automatically waive it without a formal petition

misconception_text: Supply Chain Management BUS5 142 is only relevant for operations concentrations
correct_answer: BUS5 142 is a strong elective for MIS students because ERP systems heavily involve supply chain modules

misconception_text: BUS3 162 Cross-Cultural Management has no prerequisite
correct_answer: BUS3 162 requires BUS3 160 Fundamentals of Management as a prerequisite

misconception_text: You can substitute BUS2 194 Predictive Analytics for BUS2 90 Business Statistics
correct_answer: BUS2 194 is an upper-division elective that requires BUS2 90; it does not substitute for the lower-division requirement

misconception_text: All BUS4 118x courses are equivalent and interchangeable electives
correct_answer: BUS4 118x courses cover different topics; each has its own prerequisites and some require specific prior courses

misconception_text: PHIL 186 can be taken before completing lower-division GE
correct_answer: PHIL 186 is an upper-division course; students should complete lower-division GE and major prep before enrolling

misconception_text: BUS3 191 Business Consulting counts as the MIS practicum requirement
correct_answer: BUS3 191 is a management consulting elective; the MIS practicum is specifically BUS4 119A or BUS4 119H

misconception_text: You can take BUS3 189 Strategic Management in junior year if you have 60 units
correct_answer: BUS3 189 requires all five upper-division business fundamentals courses as prerequisites regardless of unit count

misconception_text: LLD 100WB is only for international students
correct_answer: LLD 100WB is open to all business students and is one of three approved GWAR options

misconception_text: BUS2 192 Business Process Management can be taken before BUS5 140
correct_answer: BUS2 192 requires BUS5 140 Operations Management as a prerequisite

misconception_text: Digital Marketing BUS2 132 satisfies the MIS additional elective requirement
correct_answer: BUS2 132 is a marketing elective satisfying University Elective credit not the MIS concentration elective block

misconception_text: You only need ECON 1A or ECON 1B not both for major preparation
correct_answer: Both ECON 1A and ECON 1B are required separately in the major preparation block for the MIS BS

misconception_text: Online courses from SJSU do not count as units in residence
correct_answer: SJSU Online courses count as units in residence as long as you are enrolled as an SJSU student

misconception_text: All courses taken at a CSU campus count as SJSU residence units
correct_answer: Residence units must be earned through SJSU enrollment; courses at other CSU campuses do not count

misconception_text: Transfer students only need 90 more units after transfer to graduate
correct_answer: The number of additional units depends on transfer credit evaluation; 30 of the final units must be in residence

misconception_text: Community college courses numbered 100 or higher are upper-division
correct_answer: California community colleges only offer lower-division coursework; all CC courses transfer as lower-division at SJSU

misconception_text: If you transfer with an AA-T all your GE is done
correct_answer: An AA-T satisfies lower-division GE but you still need upper-division GE GWAR PE and major requirements

misconception_text: AP credit from high school automatically satisfies SJSU major prerequisites
correct_answer: AP credit may satisfy GE requirements but major prerequisites must be evaluated individually by the department

misconception_text: You can complete your final 30 units through online courses at another university
correct_answer: The final 30 units must be completed at SJSU in residence; they cannot be taken at another institution

misconception_text: Transfer credit for a database course automatically satisfies BUS4 112
correct_answer: Transfer course equivalencies for major courses must be approved by the MIS department; not automatic

misconception_text: You do not need advisor approval to substitute one major course for another
correct_answer: All major course substitutions require a formal petition approved by your department advisor

misconception_text: Taking more than 120 units automatically qualifies you for graduation
correct_answer: Unit count is one criterion; all specific graduation requirements must still be individually satisfied

misconception_text: BUS2 198 Business Capstone can replace BUS3 189 Strategic Management
correct_answer: BUS2 198 is an analytics capstone elective; BUS3 189 is a separate required course in the integration block

misconception_text: You can take the MIS practicum BUS4 119A without completing BUS4 119B
correct_answer: BUS4 119A requires BUS4 119B as a direct prerequisite; both the strategy and systems courses must come first

misconception_text: BUS3 191 Business Consulting Practicum counts as a senior capstone replacing BUS3 189
correct_answer: BUS3 191 is an elective practicum; BUS3 189 Strategic Management is still required as a separate course

misconception_text: The MIS Internship BUS4 119C satisfies the practicum requirement
correct_answer: BUS4 119C is a University Elective; only BUS4 119A or BUS4 119H satisfy the MIS practicum requirement

misconception_text: Completing BUS4 119B automatically enrolls you in the practicum
correct_answer: BUS4 119A or 119H must be registered separately; completing 119B only satisfies the prerequisite for enrollment
